body_txt: Bob is preparing to take his share of a large item (like a pie) that is clearly meant to be split between several people. He cuts a Bob-sized wedge from the main lump, places it carefully on a plate — and then leaves it behind while he makes off with the rest. The smaller the initial portion, the funnier the joke becomes: what appeared at first to be moderation and restraint on Bob's part is actually just greed , since a smaller remnant means more for him. This is most commonly seen with cake, where Bob will cut one slice and take the rest for himself. A similar sight gag is when Alice, giving a family member some spending money, pulls a few bills from her wallet and Bob makes off with the wallet. The gag can be further subverted if Bob leaves with the larger portion and then returns to get the smaller piece too. Taxman Takes the Winnings is a related gag: someone who has just come into possession of a big bundle of money is promptly accosted by the Intimidating Revenue Service , who divvy up the winnings and make off with most of it, if not all. They may even threaten Bob with prison if he doesn't cough up more ... Most often seen in Western Animation . Compare I'll Take Two Beers Too , Fight for the Last Bite , and Ate It All .
